title: Method and electronic device for rendering scalable vector graphics content
abstract: A method for rendering a SVG content is provided. The method includes identifying one or more SVG tags and attributes in the SVG content by parsing the SVG content. Further, the method includes converting each of the identified SVG tags and attributes to a unique binary stream for obtaining a binary representation of the SVG content. The unique binary stream represents each of the SVG tags and the attributes. Further, the method includes storing the binary representation of the SVG content. Further, the method includes dynamically rendering the SVG content by parsing the binary representation.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) An electronic device, comprising: at least one memory storing instructions, a scalable vector graphics (SVG) content, and a mapping table, wherein the mapping table associates each of a plurality of SVG tags with a corresponding binary stream and each of a plurality of SVG attributes with a corresponding binary stream; and at least one processor operatively coupled with the memory, configured to execute the stored instructions to: identify one or more SVG tags and attributes in the SVG content by parsing the SVG content, obtain a binary representation of the SVG content by converting each of the identified SVG tags and attributes to the corresponding binary stream by using the mapping table, and store the binary representation of the SVG content in the memory. The electronic device of claim 1, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to execute the stored instructions to: insert a unique signature tag into the binary representation of the SVG content. The electronic device of claim 1, wherein the SVG content is included in an application package, and wherein the at least one processor is further configured to execute the stored instructions to: extract the SVG content from the application package, convert the extracted SVG content to the binary representation of the SVG content, and, include the binary representation of the SVG content in the application package. An electronic device comprising: a display; a memory storing instructions and a mapping table, wherein the mapping table associates each of a plurality of scalable vector graphic (SVG) tags with a corresponding binary stream and each of a plurality of SVG attributes with a corresponding binary stream; and at least one processor operatively coupled with the memory and the display, configured to execute the stored instructions to: identify whether a SVG content is in the binary representation, upon determining that the SVG content is in a binary representation, obtain vector data of the SVG content by parsing the binary representation of the SVG content, by using the mapping table, and control the display to display the SVG content by rendering the vector data. The electronic device of claim 4, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to execute the stored instructions to: identify that the SVG content is in the binary representation by identifying a unique signature tag in the SVG content, and upon determining that the SVG content is not in the binary representation, obtain the vector data of the SVG content by parsing the SVG content. The electronic device of claim 5, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to execute the stored instructions to: upon determining that the SVG content is not in the binary representation, identify one or more SVG tags and attributes in the SVG content by parsing the SVG content, obtain the binary representation of the SVG content by converting each of the identified SVG tags and attributes to the corresponding binary stream by using the mapping table, and store the binary representation of the SVG content in the memory. The electronic device of claim 6, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to execute the stored instructions to: execute an application in the memory, the application including the SVG content, and convert the SVG content in the application package to the binary representation of the SVG content. The electronic device of claim 7, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to execute the stored instructions to: extract the SVG content from the application package, convert the extracted SVG content to the binary representation of the SVG content, and include the binary representation of the SVG content in the application package. A method for generating binary representation of scalable vector graphics (SVG) content, the method comprising: identifying one or more SVG tags and attributes in the SVG content by parsing the SVG content; obtaining a binary representation of the SVG content by converting each of the SVG tags and attributes to the corresponding binary stream by using the mapping table, wherein the mapping table associates each of the SVG tags with a corresponding binary stream and each of the SVG attributes with a corresponding binary stream; and storing the binary representation of the SVG content. The method of claim 9, further comprising: inserting a unique signature tag in the binary representation of the SVG content. The method of claim 9, wherein the SVG content is included in an application package, and wherein the method further comprises: extracting the SVG content in the application package; converting the extracted SVG content to the binary representation of the SVG content; and including the binary representation of the SVG content in the application package. A method for rendering scalable vector graphics (SVG) content, the method comprising: identifying whether the SVG content is in a binary representation; upon determining that the SVG content is in the binary representation, obtaining vector data of the SVG content by parsing the binary representation of the SVG content, by using the mapping table, wherein the mapping table associates each of a plurality of SVG tags with a corresponding binary stream and each of a plurality of SVG attributes with a corresponding binary stream; and displaying the SVG content by rendering the vector data. The method of claim 12, wherein the identifying of whether the SVG content is in the binary representation comprises identifying a unique signature tag in the SVG content, and wherein the method further comprises, based on identifying that the SVG content is not in the binary representation, obtaining vector data of the SVG content by parsing the SVG content. The method of claim 13, further comprising: upon determining that the SVG content is not in the binary representation, identifying one or more SVG tags and attributes in the SVG content by parsing the SVG content; obtaining binary representation of the SVG content by converting each of the identified SVG tags and attributes to the corresponding binary stream by using the mapping table; and storing the binary representation of the SVG content in the memory. The method of claim 14, further comprising: executing an application including the SVG content, and extracting the SVG content in the application package; converting the extracted SVG content to the binary representation of the SVG content; and including the binary representation of the SVG content in the application package.
